channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Visual impairment doesn't stop man from running sports podcast, bowling league
item.description: "One day a few of us got together and said, 'Let's start a podcast. Let's start a sports show,'" Bob Branco recalled. "We used Skype, but now we use Zoom.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bawden, NewsNation

item.title: Richmond recovers from water crisis, residents celebrate clean water
item.description: "We were spending eight hours cleaning all of the water," Nesrala said. "Eight hours just running the water, it was like milky and it was nasty.
item.dc:creator: Sara Molina, NewsNation

item.title: Oscars push back nominations announcement amid Calif. wildfires
item.description: With fires still active in the Los Angeles area, the film academy also extended the nominations voting period for its members through Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Firm has no plans to salvage more Titanic artifacts, snuffing out legal fight
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — The U.S. government has scrapped its litigation against the company that owns the salvage rights to the Titanic, noting that the firm no longer has expedition plans to the shipwreck that could break federal law.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former Disney World employee to plead guilty to hacking menus, changing allergen information: court records
item.description: A former Walt Disney World employee will plead guilty to hacking menu-creation software to include profanity and alter allergy information, court records indicate.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge clears the way for release of special counsel Smith's report on Trump's 2020 election case
item.description: A federal judge says the Justice Department can release special counsel Jack Smith’s investigative report on President-elect Donald Trump’s 2020 election interference case.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer And Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Taquitos sold at Aldi recalled after customer complains of 'dental injury': USDA
item.description: Aldi’s “product recalls” webpage does not currently list the taquitos among any currently recalled items.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Living longer can mean more dementia but there are ways to reduce the risk
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — About a million Americans a year are expected to develop dementia by 2060, roughly double today’s toll, researchers reported Monday.
item.dc:creator: LAURAN NEERGAARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How to watch Trump's inauguration on NewsNation
item.description: Donald Trump will be inaugurated as the 47th U.S. president on Jan. 20 for his second nonconsecutive term.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Millions have had student loans canceled under Biden
item.description: Despite failing to deliver his promise for broad student loan forgiveness, President Joe Biden has now overseen the cancellation of student loans for more than 5 million Americans — more than any other president in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Key Republican says he will support Gabbard as Trump DNI pick
item.description: Sen. James Lankford (R-Okla.) said in a Sunday interview that he will support President-elect Donald Trump’s pick for director of national intelligence, former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii).
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Newsom concerned Trump will try to withhold California fire aid
item.description: “He’s done it in Utah. He’s done it in Michigan, did it in Puerto Rico. He did it to California back before I was even governor, in 2018, until he found out folks in Orange County voted for him and he decided to give them money.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's inauguration weekend features fireworks, MAGA rally, inaugural balls
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration weekend will start with a fireworks display outside his northern Virginia golf club and end with a trio of inaugural balls where he will speak, the committee planning the events announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: East Tennessee authorities use snowy tracks to find Georgia fugitive
item.description: The recent snowfall across Tennessee actually helped law enforcement track down a fugitive from Georgia Sunday morning.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Guerry, NewsNation

item.title: PHOTOS: West Virginia father-son duo build ice dragon igloo
item.description: While many of us were staying cozy indoors, a father-son duo was hard at work making the best of this week's wintery weather by building a one-of-a-kind igloo.
item.dc:creator: Sam Gorski, ABC 4

item.title: Elon Musk donates Cybertrucks to provide LA community with power, Internet amidst wildfire devastation
item.description: A Los Angeles County community near the Eaton Fire is getting help as wildfires have ravaged through residents' homes throughout the past week. Altadena, a census-designated place just north of Pasadena, has experienced power outages as among the many issues wildfires in the area have caused.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New rule could slash nicotine levels in tobacco products
item.description: The Biden administration is poised to try to lower the amount of nicotine in tobacco products, an eleventh-hour effort that’s been years in the making.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden says he was the steady hand the world needed after Trump, who's ready to shake things up again
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden strode into the White House four years ago with a foreign policy agenda that put repairing alliances strained by four years of Republican Donald Trump's “America First” worldview front and center.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Houston pimp wanted for human trafficking apprehended after being admitted to hospital
item.description: A Houston man with a lengthy criminal history was located in a hospital nearly 20 months after jumping bail and skipping court, where he was sentenced for human trafficking.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A dad and daughter help evacuees learn whether their homes survived the California wildfires
item.description: As the fire roared down a hillside toward their Altadena home, Vanessa Prata and her parents hurried to pack their car. They focused on saving irreplaceable items, like family photographs and a baby doll from Vanessa's childhood. But they didn't leave.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspect arrested in 16th Street Mall stabbings that killed 2, injured 2
item.description: Denver police said a man has been arrested in connection to four stabbings along 16th Street that killed a woman and a man and injured two others over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Factor, NewsNation

item.title: California relying on prisoners, Mexico to provide firefighters to battle Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: California has deployed firefighters from Mexico as well as nearly 1,000 prison inmates to help combat deadly wildfires that have exploded across Los Angeles since last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California man says two of his cats died after drinking raw milk recalled for bird flu
item.description: A California man whose two cats died after drinking raw milk recalled for bird flu risk says he meant to keep his beloved pets healthy, but his efforts tragically backfired.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How a week of weather extremes upended the lives of millions of Americans
item.description: It was a week of fire and ice. It began with millions of people across the U.S. shivering amid blizzard conditions and frigid air that lasted for days, thanks to a jet stream that slips out of its usual path more often these days.
item.dc:creator: MELINA WALLING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ohio State getting shot at 6th national title after 28-14 win over Texas in CFP semi Cotton Bowl
item.description: Sawyer stripped Texas quarterback Quinn Ewers — his former roommate — and returned the fumble 83 yards for the clinching touchdown in a 28-14 victory over the Longhorns in the Cotton Bowl on Friday night, giving the Buckeyes a shot at their sixth AP national title.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN HAWKINS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: LA Mayor Karen Bass faces critical leadership test as questions emerge about wildfire response
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Earlier this week, as hurricane-strength winds blew through bone-dry hillside subdivisions, Los Angeles saw its worst nightmare realized as long-predicted firestorms engulfed wide swaths of the nation's second-largest city.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Disney pledges $15 million in Los Angeles fire aid as more stars learn they've lost their homes
item.description: Disney says it will donate $15 million to respond to the fires and help rebuild. The company announced the donations Friday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Newsom calls for investigation into water supply after tanks 'went dry' during wildfire
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom has ordered an investigation into the Los Angeles Department of Water and Power amid reports of a loss of water pressure to fire hydrants and limited water resources in the wildfire zones.
item.dc:creator: Eytan Wallace, Fox 5

item.title: Excerpts from President-elect Donald Trump’s sentencing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump spoke for six minutes. The judge talked for seven. In the end, the first criminal court sentencing of a former or future president took little more than half an hour.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former Miami Dolphins player arrested after crash in South Carolina
item.description: Former NFL player Robert Quinn was arrested in South Carolina following an early Friday morning crash.
item.dc:creator: Tim Renaud, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to uphold law that would force TikTok to shut down on Jan. 19
item.description: The Supreme Court on Friday seemed likely to uphold a law that would ban TikTok in the United States beginning Jan. 19 unless the popular social media program is sold by its China-based parent company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man detained near Kenneth Fire not accused of arson
item.description: Editor's Note: This article has been updated and a video removed to clarify there were no arson charges. LOS ANGELES (KTLA) - A man who was detained by citizens shortly after a wildfire exploded near Calabasas and West Hills is not accused of starting the fire, authorities announced Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, ABC 4

item.title: Macy's reveals locations of closing stores, including 9 in California
item.description: Macy's revealed the locations of 66 stores, including nine in California, that will be closing as part of the department store chain's Bold New Chapter strategy.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KTLA 5

item.title: Actor Steve Guttenberg returns to his wildfire-charred LA neighborhood
item.description: Steve Guttenberg awoke Thursday morning to a grim reality: The treacherous wildfire that tore through the Pacific Palisades had left his once-lush neighborhood charred and unrecognizable.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Wildfires latest: At least 10,000 structures have burned in a series of fires around Los Angeles
item.description: Firefighters battled Thursday to control a series of major fires in the Los Angeles area that have killed seven people, burned at least 10,000 structures from the Pacific Coast to Pasadena and sent thousands of people frantically fleeing their homes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jimmy Carter lauded for humility and service in Washington before being laid to rest in Georgia
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Jimmy Carter was celebrated Thursday for his personal humility and public service before, during and after his presidency in a funeral at Washington National Cathedral featuring the kind of pageantry the 39th U.S. president typically eschewed.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: LIVE: More evacuations ordered after fast-moving Kenneth fire ignites in Los Angeles area
item.description: Firefighters are racing to contain a new wildfire that broke out close to homes in the area of Calabasas and Hidden Hills, northwest of Los Angeles, on Thursday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, ABC 4
